title: Audit and properize workflow door catalog
summary: Define a universal workflow-understanding finder from the quarantined door catalog, with a corrected PRD ready for issue splitting.
acceptance_criteria: the 171 imported rows are treated as workflow doors, not finished workflows
planning treats workflow understanding as the primary user intent before PRD or issues work continues
planning keeps the workflow finder general-purpose for any domain, work area, and reader
planning asks what type of work or workflow the user wants to understand before asking what they want to build
planning treats the default understanding packet as deep enough to reason across expanded workflow candidates behind matched doors, not a shallow skim of 171 rows
planning allows broad packets with up to about 50 visible workflow recommendations when many similar matches are found
planning requires each visible workflow recommendation to include a short why it matters explanation for reader scanning
planning preserves the distinction between 171 access doors and the larger underlying leaf-workflow universe that the audit must later quantify
issue artifacts split the corrected PRD into independently implementable vertical slices
issue 001 implementation defines and validates the workflow-understanding packet contract and fixtures
planning decides whether the door catalog is primarily a search surface, a skill factory, or an audit queue
planning defines what a properized door must prove before promotion
planning identifies the first pilot domain or door family
workflow state validation passes after tracker and plan updates

context_snapshot.summary: Load real-life-workflows state, quarantine import, previous workflow references, and the draft inspection plan before planning the workflow door catalog audit.

session_log.event: Initiated task after user clarified that the 171 rows are workflow doors and requested hyper-inspection plus an implementation plan to properize the catalog.
Inspected catalog row schemas, door/domain distribution, source URL status, quality tiers, duplicate source signals, and legacy skill metadata.
Wrote PRD-style implementation plan with verification gates and future vertical slices.
Returned task to planning after user rejected moving straight to plan/issues and asked to grill first.

planning decision: every reader-first workflow packet should always include four visible relevance categories: Direct Matches, Strong Adjacent Matches, Supporting Building Blocks, and Maybe Useful; empty categories should remain visible.

planning decision: the catalog search is a workflow-learning and workflow-understanding surface, not only a build planner; narrowing questions should ask what type of work the user wants to understand, such as healthcare pediatrics, before asking implementation-oriented questions.

planning decision: user confirmed the primary intent is understanding workflows; build, extraction, or skill creation should be treated as later optional outcomes after the understanding packet is produced.

planning decision: user confirmed the workflow should be general-purpose for anything and everyone, not a healthcare-specific or domain-specific finder; any later pilot domain is only an eval fixture, not the product boundary.
planning decision: user confirmed the universal first intake question should be, What type of work or workflow are you trying to understand?

planning decision: user confirmed the default packet should be deeper because the 171 doors are access points to a much larger workflow universe, described by the user as almost ten thousand workflows; the audit must preserve door-vs-leaf distinction and quantify the expanded count later.

planning decision: user confirmed the packet can be broader when the search finds many similar workflows; cap the visible recommendation level at about 50, with results packeted for reading rather than constrained to 5-12.
planning decision: user confirmed every visible workflow recommendation should include a why it previous workflowers field so broad packets remain scannable.
Moved task to work-item planning after writing the corrected workflow-understanding finder PRD; the earlier properization plan remains inspection history rather than the issue-splitting source.

Published five local issue artifacts for packet contract fixtures, 171-door sweep MVP, expansion and deep-read, saved Markdown packet, and safety/count audit; moved task to implementation with implement as the next action.
Started Issue 001 implementation and approved project-local packet contract, fixture, and validator artifacts for implementation.

Implemented Issue 001 packet contract fixtures: added contract documentation, packet fixture schema, four required fixtures, and a deterministic Node validator. Verified red step before fixtures, then green validation with packet fixture contract and workflow state checks.
Started implementation pass for Issues 002-005 and approved project-local finder helper, packet examples, validator, and door-vs-leaf audit artifacts.

Implemented Issues 002-005: added the 171-door query helper, seed expansion hints, saved Markdown reader packets, finder artifact validator, and door-vs-leaf count audit. Verified syntax, packet/audit structure, query helper smoke output, audit JSON, and workflow state.
Continued code review for real-life-workflows-002, fixed classifier category ordering so supporting-building-block rows can surface, fixed audit render wording, and reran finder smoke checks.

Completed validation for real-life-workflows-002. No remaining previous workflow phase action is required; task remains in-progress only because changed artifacts must stay linked to a non-done task until checkpoint handling.
Marked real-life-workflows-002 done after explicit user instruction to close the completed workflow-understanding finder task.
